response.justification: The candidate is a strong, long-tenured individual contributor with genuine developer-tooling credentials — open-source test runners, CI/CD infrastructure, and recent MCP/agent tooling work — but this is a Level II people-management role and the resume shows no people-management or team-leadership experience at all. Technical leadership is implied by staff-level titles and a founder role rather than demonstrated through leading engineers, roadmaps, or organizational change, and there is no evidence of performance management, one-on-ones, or team development. The technical foundation is relevant, but the core requirement of 2+ years managing people is unmet.
strengths: 19+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems, far exceeding the 8-year minimum

Authentic developer-tools and testing-infrastructure background: authored test frameworks (Trial, Fluent-Asserts), built CI/CD and device-orchestration infrastructure, and currently works on MCP code generators
Strong quality and mentorship culture — TDD/craftsmanship practice, Selenium courses, workshops, and code katas show aptitude for developing other engineers
concerns: No people-management experience on the resume, against an explicit minimum of 2 years for this manager role
No demonstrated strategic roadmap ownership, organizational-change leadership, or formal technical leadership of teams
Mobile-platform exposure is limited to app development (iOS/Apple TV) rather than build systems or tooling for a large mobile ecosystem

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling and testing infrastructure expertise
